advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Ensure a failed observability materialize is retried on subsequent runs rather than being permanently lost when deployed-sha advances (e.g. persist a pending-materialize marker or gate sha-advance on OBS success).
evidence: infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh:206-208: `err obs-materialize-failed ...` (no exit / no backoff — non-fatal by design)
infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh:215: `echo "$TARGET" > "$SHA_FILE.tmp" && mv "$SHA_FILE.tmp" "$SHA_FILE"` runs unconditionally after the OBS block

infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh:145: `changed() { git -C "$MIRROR_DIR" diff --name-only "$DEPLOYED" "$TARGET" ... }` — once DEPLOYED advances to TARGET, the probe delta is gone and `changed "$OBS_PATHS"` will not fire again
advisory.finding: A non-fatal observability-materialize failure still advances deployed-sha, so the refresh is never retried; the probe stays stale after a single alarm until an unrelated future probe change.
scenarios: install-observability.sh transiently fails (e.g. daemon-reload race, systemctl unavailable, disk pressure); next run compares the new DEPLOYED..TARGET which no longer includes the probe change, so materialization is skipped and /usr/local/bin/rebar-observability.sh silently stays stale — exactly the bug this ticket fixes.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a drift check that fails when OBS_PATHS / install target drift from the installer's actual source of truth, per ticket AC #2.
evidence: show_ticket 1d63-430a-1a70-4987 AC: `A drift check (config-check or a test) fails when the materialised copy can go stale — i.e. the install path is derived from one source of truth`
tests/scripts/test_autodeploy_materialize_probe.py:117 asserts only that the installer re-runs (`autodeploy must re-run install-observability.sh ...`), not that the install path is single-sourced
ABSENCE: no change to infra/scripts/config-check.sh and no test binding OBS_PATHS to the installer's source list / install path
autodeploy.sh:207-208 `elif ! bash "$MIRROR_DIR/infra/scripts/install-observability.sh"; then` / `err obs-materialize-failed "install-observability.sh failed; /usr/local/bin probe may be stale"`
Commit message: 'non-fatal but emits an AUTODEPLOY err marker on failure so staleness is alarmed, not silent'

ABSENCE: the only test (test_autodeploy_materialize_probe.py:104) uses an installer stub that always `exit 0` (line 53) and asserts only `marker.exists()` (line 116); no test drives a non-zero installer exit, so the err-marker emission and the non-fatal-still-advances contract have no regression coverage.
advisory.finding: Ticket AC requiring a drift check (config-check or test) that fails when the materialized /usr/local/bin copy can go stale from a single source of truth is not implemented; only a re-run assertion test is added.
merged_from: The failure/non-fatal path of the new observability re-materialize block is untested: no test asserts that an install-observability.sh (or checkout) failure emits the `AUTODEPLOY_ERROR obs-materialize-failed` stderr marker while the deploy still completes non-fatally (deployed-sha advanced, review-bot not rolled back). This is a headline behavior of the change ('emits an AUTODEPLOY err marker on failure so staleness is alarmed, not silent').
scenarios: A future edit adds a new installer-materialized script or changes the /usr/local/bin path without updating OBS_PATHS; nothing fails, so the same drift class recurs.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Strengthen the test to also assert result.returncode == 0 and the success log line, not just marker existence.
evidence: tests/scripts/test_autodeploy_materialize_probe.py:116-120: only `assert marker.exists()`; `result.returncode` is printed in the message but never asserted
infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh:210 emits `host observability probe re-materialized on the box` on success — not asserted by the test
advisory.finding: The regression test asserts only marker.exists() and never checks the process return code or that the success log path was taken, so it cannot distinguish a clean materialize from one that ran the installer then errored elsewhere.
scenarios: A change that makes install-observability.sh run but then leaves autodeploy exiting non-zero or skipping the success log would still pass this test.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a negative test where the git diff stub reports no observability change and assert install-observability.sh is NOT invoked (marker absent).
evidence: autodeploy.sh:203 `if changed "$OBS_PATHS"; then`

test git stub: `case "$*" in *observability.sh*) echo "infra/scripts/observability.sh"; exit 0 ;; esac` (test file lines 76) — the substring `observability.sh` matches both OBS_PATHS entries, so the guard fires in every run
ABSENCE: no test exercises the case where the diff reports NO observability change and asserts `marker` was NOT written; the guard's false branch is untested.
advisory.finding: The changed-path guard for the observability block is not discriminated: the git stub reports a change for any diff argv containing `observability.sh`, so `changed "$OBS_PATHS"` always fires. No negative test asserts the installer does NOT run when OBS_PATHS is unchanged, so an implementation that ran install-observability.sh unconditionally would still pass.
scenarios: A refactor breaks the OBS_PATHS matching so the installer runs on every deploy (or never) — the single test cannot distinguish guarded from unconditional invocation

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Sync the probe source into $DEPLOY_REPO (or run the installer from $DEPLOY_REPO) so an obs-only deploy does not leave /opt/rebar stale while deployed-sha is advanced.
evidence: infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh:161 `if ! rsync -a --delete "${RSYNC_EXCLUDES[@]}" "$MIRROR_DIR/" "$DEPLOY_REPO/" 2>/dev/null; then` — the ONLY rsync into DEPLOY_REPO, and it is inside the BOT_PATHS block
infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh:203 `if changed "$OBS_PATHS"; then` — the OBS block checks out TARGET in the mirror and runs the installer from $MIRROR_DIR, with no rsync into $DEPLOY_REPO

infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh:215 `echo "$TARGET" > "$SHA_FILE.tmp" && mv "$SHA_FILE.tmp" "$SHA_FILE"` — deployed-sha advances to TARGET regardless, so $DEPLOY_REPO is marked deployed while its observability.sh copy is stale
infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh:22-23 header: 'bounded blast radius: NEVER touches the `gerrit` container; only the review-bot service is rebuilt/restarted; config refs are never auto-applied.'
infra/scripts/install-observability.sh:14 `cat >/etc/systemd/system/rebar-observability.service <<'UNIT'` and :38-39 `systemctl daemon-reload` / `systemctl enable --now rebar-observability.timer`
infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh:207 `elif ! bash "$MIRROR_DIR/infra/scripts/install-observability.sh"; then` — invokes the full installer, including systemd unit rewrites, in the unattended timer path
advisory.finding: On a probe-only change, the new OBS block re-materializes /usr/local/bin from $MIRROR_DIR but never rsyncs the mirror into $DEPLOY_REPO (/opt/rebar); only the BOT block rsyncs. deployed-sha is still advanced to TARGET, so the compose build-context copy at $DEPLOY_REPO/infra/scripts/observability.sh silently diverges from the recorded deployed SHA — re-introducing the staleness this change set out to eliminate, just at a different path.
merged_from: The unattended OBS path re-runs install-observability.sh, which (under `set -euo pipefail`) rewrites /etc/systemd/system unit files, runs `systemctl daemon-reload`, and `systemctl enable --now rebar-observability.timer` on every probe-source change — a wider host blast radius than the file header's stated 'only the review-bot service is rebuilt/restarted' invariant, and one that mutates systemd unit state and immediately fires the timer.
scenarios: A commit changes only infra/scripts/observability.sh; autodeploy refreshes /usr/local/bin but /opt/rebar/infra/scripts/observability.sh keeps the old content while deployed-sha reports the new SHA
advisory.suggested_fix: In the OBS block, rsync the checked-out mirror into $DEPLOY_REPO before/instead of running from $MIRROR_DIR, or run install-observability.sh from $DEPLOY_REPO/infra/scripts/ after an rsync, so the compose build context and /usr/local/bin converge together.
